rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALDSON LEO POWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. Each child receives 5.555% of the decedent's 33.33% interest in the real property, not 11.11%, and 1/6 of any after discovered separate property, not 11.1%. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distirbution". [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE BRETHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Additionally, the register of actions now reflects a "certified certificate" for another estate filed on 8/24/22, a courtesy copy of which has not been received by the assigned examiner as of the end of the day, 8/24/22. No proof of service of notice of the amended distribution appears to have been filed either. [CRC 7.53(a).] According to the register of actions, the amended distribution calculation document was not e-filed until 8/12/22, when the document was first available for viewing and after which date notice of hearing should have been given, not before. The courtesy copy of an "Assignment of Share" filed on 8/19/22 did not have any attachment. A single-sided courtesy copy of the amended petition filed on 7/28/22 and proof of service of the notice of hearing of that amended petition (and any other papers filed in the meantime) must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATE BELSER KREBSACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required by both the attorney and the personal representative, as the PR took almost a million dollars from the estate bank account and invested it in her own name, notwithstanding the petition stating under penalty of perjury that the estate savings account held $1,123,990.80. it is noted that the proposed order still has that incorrect allegation in it at paragraph 15. In addition, the closing escrow statement dated 3/14/22 shows $1,423,890.80 balance due to seller, but 2 weeks later, on 3/28/22, when the petiiton was signed and verified, the estate accounts only held $1,146,203.65, over $277,000 less than would be expected. counsel may be asked to explain why there was so much less on hand than expected. finally, counsel calculated the statutory fees by including gain on sale, but the petiiton is on waiver of account and there is no detailed schedule of receipts (see CRC 7.705(b)). removing the gain reduces the fee by $2,400, to $29,696. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSS C. GORDON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/22. No earlier date available. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The proposed order and courtesy copies must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/14/22 to avoid another automatic continuance. A cursory review of the petition online indicates that the full disclosure required by Probate Code Section 9202(b) was not made (as to any previous confinement of heirs, not just in the present). The statutory fee calculation also appears to be incorrect; however, if the correct, higher amount is requested, that would be an amendment to the petition requiring notice. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/12/22 to be heard with related petition and also for the following issues to be addressed: No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 41.59 E.] The Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties & Liabilities has not been filed. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a cover sheet. No proof of publication has been filed. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and their other issue who were the parents of the nephews and when did each of them die? The relationship of each person (whether a specific kin or "stranger") must be shown in item 8 of the petition. If full authority is to be granted but only $500 bond, all heirs must waive bond in excess of $500. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY OWYANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Removal Of Trustees, Appointment Of Receiver, For Distribution Of Trust Property, And An Accounting; Declaration Of Carroll J. Collins
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/2022. 1) The petition must be signed by Crystal Owyang. CRC 7.103(a). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) The petition must allege specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by the trustees in support of venue in San Francisco County, in light of the trustees' residence in Contra Costa County. Probate Code sections 17002(a) & 17005(a)(1). 4) The addendum to the objection filed on 08/22/2022 must be verified by at least one of the objectors. CRC 7.103(b). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONISA TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH E. SU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must submit a certified copy of the will admitted in Colorado as if it were an original Will in room 103. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 3) Petitioner did not select a required box on page 3 under #5.a. (3) or (4) 4) Petitioner must file a DE-147 Duties and Liabilities. 5) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
